text: Persist watchdog desired state and last stop reason under .bowman/runtime
description: Move the durable watchdog control state beyond active session metadata: whether watchdog is enabled, the last stop reason, emergency-stop state, and the preferred agent/runtime mode should become project-local runtime state the backend/session layer can restore and explain after reload or restart.

reflections.context: Persist watchdog desired state and last stop reason under .bowman/runtime so animator control state survives reloads and can be restored by the local runtime.
reflections.outcome: Added .bowman/runtime/watchdog-state.json persistence for enabled state, emergency-stop state, stop reason, and preferred agent type. Session runtimes now restore and emit that state, and the frontend hydrates from backend runtime APIs when not connected.
reflections.effectiveness: This worked well because the state boundary was narrow and concrete: session runtimes own live control state, the backend owns durable read/write APIs, and the frontend becomes an observer instead of the source of truth.
reflections.lesson: For autonomy work, push durable control state down into project-local runtime files early. It makes reconnect, recovery, and future scheduler ownership much easier.
reflections.pattern: This matches previous work on session metadata persistence: once runtime truth moves out of browser-local state, the UI becomes simpler and more reliable.

status.comment: Persisted watchdog desired state, emergency-stop state, preferred agent type, and last stop reason in .bowman/runtime/watchdog-state.json. Session runtimes now restore and emit that state across reconnects.
